title: MENTAL HEALTH FIRST AID TRAINING - 211info
text: NATIONAL COUNCIL FOR MENTAL WELLBEING MENTAL HEALTH FIRST AID TRAINING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a training program that teaches skills involved in identifying and responding to signs of mental illness and substance use disorders in the community, which enables individuals to provide help and support to individuals experiencing said issues. Classes can be taken in-person or virtually. 1400 K Street NW Suite 1050 Washington, DC 20005 Eligibility: Age 14 and older Hours: Varies by course Email (202) 684-7457 3 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (202) 684-7457 ext 3 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Nationwide Services: First Aid Instruction * Mental Health Issues First Aid Instruction * Substance Use Disorder Issues If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. NATIONAL COUNCIL FOR MENTAL WELLBEING
